title: Solo-Dinner Braise Uruguayan Smoke-Hot Earthy
description: A hearty, earthy, solo-dinner Uruguayan dish featuring sake, smoke-hot flavors and Ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly ingredients. Serve on a beautiful Talavera plate.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Brown the short ribs on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the short ribs from the pot and set them aside.
Reduce the heat to medium and sauté the chopped onion until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1-2 minutes.
Add the sake, smoked paprika, ground cumin, and chili powder. Stir to combine.
Return the short ribs to the pot and cover with a lid.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and braise the short ribs for 2 1/2 hours, or until tender.
Season the short ribs with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the short ribs on a beautiful Talavera plate, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
